title: Preserve Navigation Context When Drilling into Object Views
story: As a data migration specialist, I want the application to preserve my current tab context when I drill from the migration view into an object, so that I land on the same tab I was working in rather than the default Dictionary view.
description:
When drilling from the Migration view into a specific object (e.g., Account), the application was navigating to the default Dictionary tab instead of preserving the Migration context. This broke the user's workflow continuity.

The solution uses a URL parameter (`view=migration`) to indicate which tab context the user came from, ensuring the object page opens to the correct tab.

### Expected Behavior
Clicking an object link from the Migration view navigates to `object.php?object=Account&view=migration`, landing the user on the Migration tab of that object.
acceptance_criteria: Drilling from Migration view into an object opens the object on the Migration tab
URL includes a `view` parameter that controls which tab is active on load
Default behavior without the parameter still opens the Dictionary tab
Back navigation returns the user to their previous position in the Migration view
ux_changes: Object drill-down links from the Migration view now include a `view` parameter that preserves the user's tab context, landing them on the Migration tab instead of the default Dictionary tab.
middleware_changes: Object page reads the `view` URL parameter and activates the corresponding tab on page load.
implementation_notes: Implemented via a URL query parameter `view=migration` appended to drill-down links. The object page checks for this parameter on load and switches to the appropriate tab.
comment_summary: Developer completed the implementation and requested review. No further discussion.
